Task: File System Management Script

Create a shell script named 'solution.sh' that performs the following operations:

1. Directory Setup:
   - Create two root directories: 'sourceDir' and 'destinationDir'
   - Within 'sourceDir', create at least 3 subdirectories at any depth
   (Example structure: sourceDir/dir1, sourceDir/dir2, sourceDir/dir1/subdir)

2. File Generation:
   - Create at least 10 regular text files distributed across the directories
   - Create at least 5 gzipped (.gz) files in any of the directories
   - Files can contain any content, but must be valid text files
   - Gzipped files must contain valid, readable text when decompressed

3. File Operations:
   - Copy the entire directory structure from 'sourceDir' to 'destinationDir'
   - Copy all regular (non-gzipped) files from 'sourceDir' to 'destinationDir', maintaining their exact paths
   - For each .gz file in 'sourceDir', create a decompressed version in 'destinationDir' at the same relative path
   Example: if sourceDir/dir1/file.txt.gz exists, create destinationDir/dir1/file.txt with the decompressed content

4. Requirements:
   - The script must preserve file contents exactly (byte-for-byte copies)
   - Directory structure in 'destinationDir' must exactly match 'sourceDir'
   - Decompressed files must exactly match their original content before compression
   - The script must exit with status code 0 on successful completion
   - All files and directories should have standard read/write permissions

Example final structure:
sourceDir/
  file1.txt
  dir1/
    file2.txt
    file3.txt.gz
  dir2/
    subdir/
      file4.txt.gz

destinationDir/
  file1.txt
  dir1/
    file2.txt
    file3.txt
  dir2/
    subdir/
      file4.txt

Task: File System Management Script

Create a shell script named 'solution.sh' that performs the following operations:

1. Directory Setup:
   - Create two root directories: 'sourceDir' and 'destinationDir'
   - Within 'sourceDir', create at least 3 subdirectories at any depth
   (Example structure: sourceDir/dir1, sourceDir/dir2, sourceDir/dir1/subdir)

2. File Generation:
   - Create at least 10 regular text files distributed across the directories
   - Create at least 5 gzipped (.gz) files in any of the directories
   - Files can contain any content, but must be valid text files
   - Gzipped files must contain valid, readable text when decompressed

3. File Operations:
   - Copy the entire directory structure from 'sourceDir' to 'destinationDir'
   - Copy all regular (non-gzipped) files from 'sourceDir' to 'destinationDir', maintaining their exact paths
   - For each .gz file in 'sourceDir', create a decompressed version in 'destinationDir' at the same relative path
   Example: if sourceDir/dir1/file.txt.gz exists, create destinationDir/dir1/file.txt with the decompressed content

4. Requirements:
   - The script must preserve file contents exactly (byte-for-byte copies)
   - Directory structure in 'destinationDir' must exactly match 'sourceDir'
   - Decompressed files must exactly match their original content before compression
   - The script must exit with status code 0 on successful completion
   - All files and directories should have standard read/write permissions

Example final structure:
sourceDir/
  file1.txt
  dir1/
    file2.txt
    file3.txt.gz
  dir2/
    subdir/
      file4.txt.gz

destinationDir/
  file1.txt
  dir1/
    file2.txt
    file3.txt
  dir2/
    subdir/
      file4.txt

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

```bash
 done
```

cd sourceDir
find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all regular files (non-gzipped) maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress each gzipped file into destinationDir with the same relative path, removing the .gz extension
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

Task: File System Management Script

Create a shell script named 'solution.sh' that performs the following operations:

1. Directory Setup:
   - Create two root directories: 'sourceDir' and 'destinationDir'
   - Within 'sourceDir', create at least 3 subdirectories at any depth
   (Example structure: sourceDir/dir1, sourceDir/dir2, sourceDir/dir1/subdir)

2. File Generation:
   - Create at least 10 regular text files distributed across the directories
   - Create at least 5 gzipped (.gz) files in any of the directories
   - Files can contain any content, but must be valid text files
   - Gzipped files must contain valid, readable text when decompressed

3. File Operations:
   - Copy the entire directory structure from 'sourceDir' to 'destinationDir'
   - Copy all regular (non-gzipped) files from 'sourceDir' to 'destinationDir', maintaining their exact paths
   - For each .gz file in 'sourceDir', create a decompressed version in 'destinationDir' at the same relative path
   Example: if sourceDir/dir1/file.txt.gz exists, create destinationDir/dir1/file.txt with the decompressed content

4. Requirements:
   - The script must preserve file contents exactly (byte-for-byte copies)
   - Directory structure in 'destinationDir' must exactly match 'sourceDir'
   - Decompressed files must exactly match their original content before compression
   - The script must exit with status code 0 on successful completion
   - All files and directories should have standard read/write permissions

sourceDir/
  file1.txt
  dir1/

    file3.txt.gz
  dir2/
    subdir/
      file4.txt.gz

destinationDir/
  file1.txt
  dir1/
    file2.txt
    file3.txt
  dir2/
    subdir/
      file4.txt

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure exactly from sourceDir to destinationDir
cd sourceDir
find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all regular files (non-gzipped) maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress each gzipped file into destinationDir with the same relative path, removing the .gz extension
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure exactly from sourceDir to destinationDir
cd sourceDir
find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all regular files (non-gzipped) maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress each gzipped file into destinationDir with the same relative path, removing the .gz extension
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure exactly from sourceDir to destinationDir
cd sourceDir
find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all regular files (non-gzipped) maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress each gzipped file into destinationDir with the same relative path, removing the .gz extension
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

tools.name: python
tools.description:
Use the python function to execute Python code.

The Python tool executes single-run Python scripts. Important notes:
1. Each execution is independent - no state is preserved between runs
2. You must explicitly use print() statements to see any output
3. Simply writing expressions (like in notebooks) will not display results
4. The script cannot accept interactive input during execution
5. Return statements alone won't produce visible output
6. All variables and imports are cleared between executions
7. Standard output (via print()) is the only way to see results

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy only the directory structure from sourceDir to destinationDir
rsync -a -f "+ */" -f "- *" sourceDir/ destinationDir/

# Copy all non-gzipped files maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' | while read filepath; do
  relpath=${filepath#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/$relpath"
  cp "$filepath" "$destfile"
done

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with same relative path but removing .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"

done

exit 0

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir
```

# Copy only the directory structure from sourceDir to destinationDir
rsync -a -f "+ */" -f "- *" sourceDir/ destinationDir/

# Copy all non-gzipped files maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' | while read filepath; do
  relpath=${filepath#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/$relpath"
  cp "$filepath" "$destfile"
done

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with same relative path but removing .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

Use the python function to execute Python code.

The Python tool executes single-run Python scripts. Important notes:
1. Each execution is independent - no state is preserved between runs

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure exactly from sourceDir to destinationDir
cd sourceDir
find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all regular files (non-gzipped) maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress each gzipped file into destinationDir with the same relative path, removing the .gz extension
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy only the directory structure from sourceDir to destinationDir
rsync -a -f "+ */" -f "- *" sourceDir/ destinationDir/

# Copy all non-gzipped files maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' | while read filepath; do
  relpath=${filepath#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/$relpath"
  cp "$filepath" "$destfile"
done

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with same relative path but removing .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

Use the python function to execute Python code.

The Python tool executes single-run Python scripts. Important notes:

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure exactly from sourceDir to destinationDir
cd sourceDir
find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all regular files (non-gzipped) maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress each gzipped file into destinationDir with the same relative path, removing the .gz extension
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir
```

# Copy only the directory structure from sourceDir to destinationDir
rsync -a -f "+ */" -f "- *" sourceDir/ destinationDir/

# Copy all non-gzipped files maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' | while read filepath; do
  relpath=${filepath#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/$relpath"
  cp "$filepath" "$destfile"
done

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with same relative path but removing .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

tools.name: python
tools.description:
Use the python function to execute Python code.

The Python tool executes single-run Python scripts. Important notes:
1. Each execution is independent - no state is preserved between runs
2. You must explicitly use print() statements to see any output
3. Simply writing expressions (like in notebooks) will not display results
4. The script cannot accept interactive input during execution
5. Return statements alone won't produce visible output
6. All variables and imports are cleared between executions
7. Standard output (via print()) is the only way to see results

#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
```
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy only the directory structure from sourceDir to destinationDir
rsync -a -f "+ */" -f "- *" sourceDir/ destinationDir/

# Copy all non-gzipped files maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' | while read filepath; do
  relpath=${filepath#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/$relpath"

done

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with same relative path but removing .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

Use the python function to execute Python code.

The Python tool executes single-run Python scripts. Important notes:
1. Each execution is independent - no state is preserved between runs
2. You must explicitly use print() statements to see any output
3. Simply writing expressions (like in notebooks) will not display results
4. The script cannot accept interactive input during execution
5. Return statements alone won't produce visible output
6. All variables and imports are cleared between executions
7. Standard output (via print()) is the only way to see results

  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

tools.name: bash
tools.description: Use this function to execute bash commands.

tools.name: python
tools.description:
Use the python function to execute Python code.

The Python tool executes single-run Python scripts. Important notes:
1. Each execution is independent - no state is preserved between runs
2. You must explicitly use print() statements to see any output
3. Simply writing expressions (like in notebooks) will not display results
4. The script cannot accept interactive input during execution
5. Return statements alone won't produce visible output
6. All variables and imports are cleared between executions
7. Standard output (via print()) is the only way to see results

tools.name: create_file
tools.description:
Create a file with the given content at the given path.

For Python files, syntax will be checked before writing.

file_path.description: The path of the file to be created.

tools.name: view_file
tools.description:
Opens the file at the given path for viewing.

Displays the file with line numbers to enable line number-based editing.

path.description: the path to the file to open.

tools.name: insert_str_file
tools.description: Insert a string into a path at a given line.

path.description: the path to the file to edit.

insert_line.description: the line to insert after. 0 inserts at the beginning of the file. Setting this to the number of lines in the file inserts at the end.

tools.name: replace_str_file
tools.description: Find and replace all occurrences of a string in a file.

path.description: the path to the file to edit.

tools.description: Runs the tests in the sandbox.

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

```bash
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir

  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then

  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
```
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else

  fi
 done

for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure exactly from sourceDir to destinationDir
cd sourceDir
find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all regular files (non-gzipped) maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress each gzipped file into destinationDir with the same relative path, removing the .gz extension
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure exactly from sourceDir to destinationDir
cd sourceDir
find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all regular files (non-gzipped) maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress each gzipped file into destinationDir with the same relative path, removing the .gz extension
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
```
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt

  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

The Python tool executes single-run Python scripts. Important notes:

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then

  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir

  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure exactly from sourceDir to destinationDir
cd sourceDir
find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all regular files (non-gzipped) maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress each gzipped file into destinationDir with the same relative path, removing the .gz extension
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0


#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure exactly from sourceDir to destinationDir
cd sourceDir
find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all regular files (non-gzipped) maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress each gzipped file into destinationDir with the same relative path, removing the .gz extension
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir
```

# Copy only the directory structure from sourceDir to destinationDir
rsync -a -f "+ */" -f "- *" sourceDir/ destinationDir/

# Copy all non-gzipped files maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' | while read filepath; do

done

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with same relative path but removing .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Create directory structure in destinationDir same as sourceDir
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all non-gzipped files maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' | while read filepath; do
  relpath=${filepath#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/$relpath"
  cp "$filepath" "$destfile"
done

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with same relative path but removing .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

```bash
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0
```

#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy only the directory structure from sourceDir to destinationDir
rsync -a -f "+ */" -f "- *" sourceDir/ destinationDir/

# Copy all non-gzipped files maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' | while read filepath; do
  relpath=${filepath#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/$relpath"
  cp "$filepath" "$destfile"
done

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with same relative path but removing .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

Use the python function to execute Python code.

The Python tool executes single-run Python scripts. Important notes:

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure exactly from sourceDir to destinationDir
cd sourceDir
find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all regular files (non-gzipped) maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress each gzipped file into destinationDir with the same relative path, removing the .gz extension
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi

for j in {1..5}; do

  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

mkdir -p destinationDir
```

# Copy all non-gzipped files maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' | while read filepath; do
  relpath=${filepath#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/$relpath"
  cp "$filepath" "$destfile"
done

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with same relative path but removing .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Create directory structure in destinationDir same as sourceDir
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all non-gzipped files maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' | while read filepath; do
  relpath=${filepath#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/$relpath"
  cp "$filepath" "$destfile"
done

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with same relative path but removing .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0

Task: File System Management Script

Create a shell script named 'solution.sh' that performs the following operations:

1. Directory Setup:
   - Create two root directories: 'sourceDir' and 'destinationDir'
   - Within 'sourceDir', create at least 3 subdirectories at any depth
   (Example structure: sourceDir/dir1, sourceDir/dir2, sourceDir/dir1/subdir)

2. File Generation:
   - Create at least 10 regular text files distributed across the directories
   - Create at least 5 gzipped (.gz) files in any of the directories
   - Files can contain any content, but must be valid text files
   - Gzipped files must contain valid, readable text when decompressed

3. File Operations:
   - Copy the entire directory structure from 'sourceDir' to 'destinationDir'
   - Copy all regular (non-gzipped) files from 'sourceDir' to 'destinationDir', maintaining their exact paths
   - For each .gz file in 'sourceDir', create a decompressed version in 'destinationDir' at the same relative path
   Example: if sourceDir/dir1/file.txt.gz exists, create destinationDir/dir1/file.txt with the decompressed content

4. Requirements:
   - The script must preserve file contents exactly (byte-for-byte copies)
   - Directory structure in 'destinationDir' must exactly match 'sourceDir'
   - Decompressed files must exactly match their original content before compression
   - The script must exit with status code 0 on successful completion
   - All files and directories should have standard read/write permissions

Example final structure:
sourceDir/
  file1.txt
  dir1/
    file2.txt
    file3.txt.gz
  dir2/
    subdir/
      file4.txt.gz

destinationDir/
  file1.txt
  dir1/
    file2.txt
    file3.txt
  dir2/
    subdir/
      file4.txt

attachments.ee5d70e3ee5111c57ca3596973b74211:
{"file_path":"solution.sh","content":"#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0
"}

attachments.231d7c8255e404c51537433fbd681137:
{"file_path":"solution.sh","content":"#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure exactly from sourceDir to destinationDir
cd sourceDir
find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all regular files (non-gzipped) maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress each gzipped file into destinationDir with the same relative path, removing the .gz extension
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0
"}

attachments.42fa0ac34fe188a5e69e7fc6515fffc6:
The script still creates an unwanted "sourceDir" directory inside "destinationDir," which is not what we want. Instead, "destinationDir" should mirror the structure inside "sourceDir," not include an extra "sourceDir" folder.

The root cause is that the usage of cp with --parents includes the full sourceDir in the path when copying files, resulting in destinationDir/sourceDir/...

To fix it, we need to:
- Copy directory structure without nesting an extra "sourceDir"
- Copy files by calculating relative paths to "sourceDir" and copying them accordingly
- Use rsync or find with cp avoiding the --parents flag, instead creating destination folders first and copying files there

I'll rewrite the script to:
- Use rsync for copying directory structures without files
- Copy txt files by finding them and copying to corresponding destination paths
- Decompress .gz files similarly
- Ensure no nesting of extra sourceDir directory in destinationDir

This should meet all requirements successfully. I'll make the changes now.
attachments.d61b1456a7678aa8e43f2915d81f23d1:
{"file_path":"solution.sh","content":"#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy only the directory structure from sourceDir to destinationDir
rsync -a -f "+ */" -f "- *" sourceDir/ destinationDir/

# Copy all non-gzipped files maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' | while read filepath; do
  relpath=${filepath#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/$relpath"
  cp "$filepath" "$destfile"
done

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with same relative path but removing .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0
"}

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

  relpath=${filepath#sourceDir/}

  cp "$filepath" "$destfile"
done

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with same relative path but removing .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0
"}

attachments.ce3ea4a1c87cbebc8a4834f262702651:
{"file_path": "solution.sh", "content": "#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files spread over different directories
for i in {1..10}; do
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir1" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir2" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 9 ]; then
    echo "Content text file $i in dir3" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content text file $i at source root" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  fi
 done

# Create 5 gzipped files in sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
 done

# Step 3: File Operations
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Create directory structure in destinationDir same as sourceDir
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy all non-gzipped files maintaining paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' | while read filepath; do
  relpath=${filepath#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/$relpath"
  cp "$filepath" "$destfile"
done

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with same relative path but removing .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  relpath=${gzfile#sourceDir/}
  destfile="destinationDir/${relpath%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0
"}

attachments.edb36a46bd3e20a720e24e8f0acc1b2e:
#!/bin/bash

# Step 1: Directory Setup
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir2
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir3

# Step 2: File Generation
# Create 10 text files
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "This is file $i" > sourceDir/file$i.txt
  sleep 0.1
  # To distribute files
  if [ $i -le 3 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir1 file $i" > sourceDir/dir1/file$i.txt
  elif [ $i -le 6 ]; then
    echo "Content for dir2 file $i" > sourceDir/dir2/file$i.txt
  else
    echo "Content for dir3 file $i" > sourceDir/dir3/file$i.txt
  fi
  
done

# Create 5 gzipped files
# Create content for gzipped files first
mkdir -p sourceDir/dir1/subdir
for j in {1..5}; do
  echo "This is gzipped file $j" > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  gzip -c sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt > sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt.gz
  rm sourceDir/dir1/subdir/file$j.txt
  sleep 0.1
done

# Step 3: File Operations
# Create destinationDir
mkdir -p destinationDir

# Copy directory structure
cd sourceDir && find . -type d -exec mkdir -p ../destinationDir/{} \;
cd ..

# Copy regular (non-gzipped) files, preserving paths
find sourceDir -type f ! -name '*.gz' -exec cp --parents {} destinationDir/ \;

# Decompress .gz files into destinationDir with the same relative path but without .gz
find sourceDir -type f -name '*.gz' | while read gzfile; do
  destfile="destinationDir/${gzfile#sourceDir/}"
  destfile="${destfile%.gz}"
  gzip -dc "$gzfile" > "$destfile"
done

exit 0
